note: Untracked files in working directory (from a previous session), not committed to git. These caused pre-existing spotless violations that I fixed via spotlessApply.
These are modified in the working tree relative to HEAD but aren't part of any current step. They should be committed in the appropriate step.

The working tree's PromptEvalRunner.java (all 6 eval types) is not committed to HEAD — HEAD only has the first 2 eval types. The working tree contains the full implementation that includes rcdo-suggest, risk-signal, reconcile-assist, and rag-query methods. These are likely from a prior session and need to be committed.

A targeted `./gradlew test --tests 'com.weeklycommit.ai.eval.PromptEvalRunner' -Dinclude.tags=eval` invocation returned “No tests found”; the normal required `build` check still passed, so this looks like an eval-task/filtering setup nuance rather than a step-1 defect.

callJudge() creates a new HttpClient instance per invocation. For the eval runner this is fine (low call volume), but if call frequency increased it would be better to reuse a single instance. Out of scope for this step.

The title judge is called whenever suggestedTitle is non-null/non-blank in the output, even for cases where shouldSuggestTitle=false. This is intentional per the step description but means judge scores will be collected for cases like draft-002 where the original title was already good and the model may suggest nothing (or null). The guard on `!output.get("suggestedTitle").isNull() && !...isBlank()` correctly handles the null/blank cases.

The health-check flags a likely cwd bug in the planned npm test chaining (`cd backend && ./gradlew test && node --test ...`). That is outside this step’s scope, but should be fixed when step 6 is implemented.

eval-baseline.json has draft-007 as the one failing case (suggestTitle=false). The model returned null for suggestedTitle on a case that expected a shortened title suggestion. This is a known LLM limitation — the enforcement script should treat this single failure as a regression anchor, not a green baseline.
The committed baseline differs from the source file only by a trailing newline at EOF. Content is otherwise identical.
The health-check’s cwd warning about `cd backend && ./gradlew test && node --test ...` still looks valid and should be handled when step 6 is implemented.

The `overall_case_pass_rate` threshold of 95.0% will hard-fail any run where draft-007 fails (which is 1/18 = 94.4%). The team may want to lower this threshold or fix draft-007's expected behavior before using this as a hard gate in CI. Out of scope for this step.

The script's `findLatestEvalResult()` picks the lexicographically last filename. Since filenames use the pattern `eval-<ISO-timestamp>.json` with colons replaced by dashes, this sorts correctly — but if any non-timestamp `.json` files were dropped in that directory, they could be picked erroneously. Out of scope.

The current baseline still hard-fails `overall_case_pass_rate` at 94.4% vs required 95.0%. That appears intentional for now, but CI will block once this script is wired in unless the baseline, thresholds, or failing case behavior changes.
The health-check warning about `cd backend && ./gradlew test && node --test ...` still appears valid and should be fixed when step 6 is implemented.
The health-check warning about appending `node --test ...` after `cd backend && ./gradlew test` still appears valid and should be handled when step 6 is implemented.
Test runs emit many React `act(...)` warnings, but the suite still passes. These appear pre-existing and outside this step’s scope.
